questions.hint_text: Positive numbers are greater than 0; compare fractions/decimals by value.
questions.explanation_text: 1/3 is positive and greater than 0, so it is the greatest here.

questions.hint_text: Negatives are less than 0. Among negatives, farther left is smaller.

questions.question_text: True or False: -3 is greater than -1.
questions.hint_text: On the number line, numbers to the right are greater.
questions.explanation_text: -3 is left of -1, so it is smaller.

questions.question_text: A point is at -4. What is its distance from 3?

questions.hint_text: For negatives, bigger absolute value means smaller number.

questions.explanation_text: 0.98 is 0.02 away from 1 (same as 1.02), and closer than 0.89.
Hundredths digit is 8, so 6.4 rounds up to 6.5.

questions.question_text: Estimate 49 × 21 by rounding to the nearest tens.
An exact answer is 198. Which estimate is most reasonable?
Explain briefly why estimation is useful when checking answers.
questions.hint_text: Think: does the answer size make sense?
questions.explanation_text: Estimates help you see if an exact answer is too big or too small.

questions.question_text: A submarine is at −120 m and rises 35 m. New position?

questions.hint_text: Subtracting makes the number smaller (more negative).

questions.question_text: Temperature was −2°C and rose 7°C. New temperature?
A diver goes down 5 m each minute for 6 minutes. Change in position?

questions.hint_text: Plug in x = −3 and compute each.

questions.correct_answer: Because a positive times a negative is negative
questions.explanation_text: A positive times a negative gives a negative product.

questions.hint_text: Pick B for this demo diagnostic.
questions.explanation_text: This is an extra diagnostic item to vary difficulty and triggers in the demo.

questions.hint_text: Multiply top and bottom by the same number.

questions.question_text: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. You eat 3 slices. What fraction did you eat?
True or False: 3/5 is the same as 3 ÷ 5.

questions.explanation_text: 1−5/6=1/6 and 1−3/4=1/4; 1/6 is smaller so 5/6 is closer.

questions.hint_text: Compare to 1: 5/6 is 1/6 away; 7/9 is 2/9 away.
questions.explanation_text: 1/6 ≈ 0.167 and 2/9 ≈ 0.222, so 5/6 is closer to 1 and larger.

A test has 80 questions. You got 75% correct. How many correct?
A phone costs $200 and sales tax is 8%. What is the tax amount?

A value goes from 40 to 30. Which is correct?

questions.explanation_text: Percent change compares the change to the original amount.

questions.hint_text: Part-to-part compares two parts, not part to whole.

questions.explanation_text: Equivalent ratios can be made by multiplying/dividing both parts by the same number.

questions.correct_answer: It shows how drawing lengths compare to real lengths
questions.explanation_text: A scale tells how much real lengths are reduced or enlarged in a drawing.

questions.question_text: What is the measure of a straight angle?
Two angles are complementary. One is 35°. What is the other?
Two angles are supplementary. One is 110°. What is the other?
A rectangle is 8 cm by 3 cm. What is its perimeter?
A rectangle is 8 cm by 3 cm. What is its area?
A triangle has base 10 cm and height 6 cm. What is its area?
Explain the difference between area and perimeter.
questions.correct_answer: Area is inside; perimeter is boundary
questions.explanation_text: Area measures the inside space; perimeter measures the distance around.

questions.question_text: A circle has radius 5 cm. What is its diameter?
Name the line segment from the center to the circle.
Use π≈3.14. A circle has diameter 10 cm. Estimate its circumference.
If the radius doubles, the circumference:
Find the volume of a box 4 cm × 3 cm × 5 cm.
A rectangular prism has base 6 cm by 2 cm and height 5 cm. Volume?
Explain in one sentence what volume means.
questions.correct_answer: How much space an object takes up
questions.explanation_text: Volume is the amount of space inside or taken up by a 3D object.
